title: The Cambridge MS (University Library, Gg. 4.27) of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned CME XML copy of University of Michigan item AGZ8234.0001.001, a Level 1, uncorrected-OCR encoding derived from an item-specific 738-page image sequence of Frederick J. Furnivall's 1868–1879 Chaucer Society edition. Furnivall transcribes Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27(1) as his Canterbury Tales base but marks exact supplied passages from Harley 1758, Sloane 1685, Dd.4.24, Harley 7335, Royal 18 C II, Royal 17 D XV, Hatton donat. 1, and an unresolved witness identified only as Z, E, or I; quotes a French source passage from Royal 19 C XI; retains Thomas Tyrwhitt's prose paragraphing; and appends Gamelyn plus woodcuts based on Ellesmere and Gg. Complete provider objects for Gg.4.27(1), Dd.4.24, Gg.4.27(2), and Ellesmere, a complete scan of Furnivall, Internet Archive support files, the complete Dd.4.24 transcription bundle, and Digital Bodleian's complete seven-canvas Hatton selection are cached in Git-ignored source-cache files with exact URLs, checksums, and target-work locators or an explicit target-absent statement; no stable U-M bulk page-image download was verified.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual unit is Furnivall's manuscript-specific composite representation, not the abstract Canterbury Tales tradition, the complete Gg.4.27(1) anthology, the printed carrier, or either digital encoding.

Gg.4.27(1) is the base witness, not the sole textual source. The relation locators audit every named source switch, supplied bracket, and comparison-only note in the CME XML; fragmented supplements are not flattened into continuous witness runs.

Gg.4.27(2) is a separately bound fourteen-leaf quire that does not contain this Canterbury Tales text. Its complete 40-canvas CUDL object is cached as evidence for the present object boundary, with target_work_presence set to absent; it is not a missing portion of part (1).

The Gg.4.27(1) CUDL cache retains all 1,144 provider canvases; the target Canterbury Tales is at fols. 132r–443v, canvases 275–902. The Dd.4.24 cache retains all 377 provider canvases, with relevant supplied passages at ff. 105v–106r (canvases 178–179) and f. 147r (canvas 257). The Huntington cache retains all 480 provider canvases; twenty-three exact portrait canvases support Furnivall's visual appendix.

Gamelyn is preserved as a spurious, non-Chaucerian appendix and does not increase the conventional count of twenty-four Chaucerian tales. The woodcut suites are visual apparatus, not textual witnesses to additional tales.
Royal 19 C XI supplies the French quotation printed before Melibee paragraph 2625; it is a supporting French source passage, not an English Canterbury Tales base witness.

Public access, local download status, underlying public-domain text, licenses for modern images or transcriptions, and provider publication terms are recorded separately. Cached files remain Git-ignored and are not thereby licensed for repository redistribution.

entities.label: University of Michigan digital text AGZ8234.0001.001
entities.description: The public U-M Corpus of Middle English item underlying the pinned XML: an uncorrected, Level 1 TEI encoding made from OCR and linked to page images. It serializes the title matter, Furnivall's edited Canterbury Tales, and Gamelyn appendix while placing broad visual and other front/back matter in omitted divisions.
notes: The 13 top-level divisions comprise an omitted-front division, ten reordered Canterbury fragments, a Gamelyn appendix, and an omitted-back division; they are digital structure, not thirteen homogeneous intellectual works.

entities.label: Furnivall's Cambridge Gg.4.27 one-text edition of The Canterbury Tales
entities.description: The Chaucer Society one-manuscript edition edited by Frederick J. Furnivall. Its Gg/Furnivall order differs from the modern fragment-number order; the edited text is composite because named passages are supplied from seven identified manuscripts plus an unresolved witness labeled Z, E, or I, and paragraph formatting in the prose tales follows Tyrwhitt.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27(1)
alternate_labels: Anthology of Geoffrey Chaucer's literary works, with other texts
entities.description: The target-bearing part of a major Chaucer anthology, compiled in East Anglia, probably near Cambridge. Its Canterbury Tales occupies fols. 132r–443v. Many illustrated leaves were removed in the sixteenth century; Joseph Holland supplied replacement leaves around 1600 from Thomas Speght's 1598 print.

date_statements.label: production of the main manuscript
date_statements.notes: Current provider dating; a cited Binski and Zutshi assessment narrows this to about 1420–1430.

holding.notes: Gg.4.27(2) is a distinct, separately bound fourteen-leaf quire and is outside this target-bearing witness entity.

physical_description.support: Primarily parchment, with later replacement leaves
notes: The complete public provider object has 1,144 canvases; provider-object extent and physical codicological extent are not conflated.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete 1,144-canvas presentation of MS Gg.4.27(1)
entities.description: The complete provider-published CUDL object, including the entire current Gg.4.27(1) anthology rather than a crop of the Canterbury Tales portion.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27(2)
entities.description: A separately bound fourteen-leaf parchment quire historically associated with Gg.4.27(1). It contains Floris and Blancheflour, King Horn, and the opening of the Assumption of the Virgin, but no portion of this Canterbury Tales work.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fourteenth century

holding.notes: Historically bound after part (1), but now a separate codicological and digital object; current binding dates to 1983.

notes: Floris and Blancheflour ff. 1r–5v; King Horn ff. 6r–13r; opening of the Assumption of the Virgin ff. 13v–14v.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete 40-canvas presentation of MS Gg.4.27(2)
entities.description: The complete current CUDL object for the separately bound quire. It is retained to document the historic/current Gg.4.27 object boundary and has verified zero target-work overlap.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1758
entities.description: A fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used repeatedly across Furnivall's edition: for the General Prologue opening; portions of the Man of Law, Friar, Summoner, Clerk, Squire, Thopas, Melibee, Monk, Nun's Priest, Canon's Yeoman, Manciple, and Parson sequences; and the Retraction. Exact discontiguous source runs are recorded in the transmission relation.

physical_description.extent: 1* + 232 folios; Canterbury Tales at ff. 1r–231r, including the Retraction

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 1685
entities.description: A fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used extensively by Furnivall for supplied links, proems, the Cook's Tale, and the spurious Gamelyn appendix.

physical_description.extent: 223 folios; Canterbury Tales at ff. 1r–222v and Gamelyn at ff. 51v–62v

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Hatton donat. 1
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript. Furnivall quotes Franklin-link lines F 679–680 from this witness; the current Bodleian catalog places the complete Squire–Franklin Link on f. 85r.

notes: The current catalog locates the Squire–Franklin Link at f. 85r.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian seven-canvas selected presentation of MS Hatton donat. 1
entities.description: A provider-published selection of seven photographed manuscript openings. It does not include f. 85r or Furnivall's two-line Franklin-link supply locus and is not a complete facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with seven JPEG images from 35mm slides

entities.label: Unresolved Furnivall witness ‘Z, E, or I’
entities.description: Furnivall tentatively labels the supplied Pardoner-link passage ‘? MS Z, E, or I.’ The checked edition does not resolve which siglum supplied the reading, so this entity preserves the uncertainty rather than assigning it to a named manuscript.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: A Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall for the Merchant's Prologue and six lines of the Pardoner's Tale (lines 743–748 inclusive). Cambridge Digital Library publishes a complete 377-canvas facsimile, while the Chaucer Manuscripts project supplies a complete downloadable diplomatic-transcription bundle.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete 377-canvas presentation of MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: The complete current provider-published CUDL object, including both Furnivall supply loci at ff. 105v–106r and f. 147r rather than crops of those passages.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 7335
entities.description: A fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall for the Merchant end-link and Squire head-link.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 C II
entities.description: A fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall for the Melibee Prologue and opening after Sir Thopas, and compared for the Retraction although Harley 1758 supplies the printed ending.

physical_description.extent: i + 272 folios; Canterbury Tales at ff. 3r–271r

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 D XV
entities.description: A later fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall for Wife of Bath's Preamble lines 717–720, Gamelyn lines 827–902, and the Nun's Priest end-link printed in his appendix.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 19 C XI
entities.description: An early fifteenth-century French manuscript containing the prose Livre de Melibee et de Prudence. Furnivall quotes a French passage from f. 59v, column 2, before returning to the English Gg text at Melibee paragraph 2625; this is supporting source evidence, not an English Canterbury Tales base witness.

notes: Livre de Melibee et de Prudence begins at f. 52r; Furnivall's quoted passage is at f. 59v, column 2.

entities.label: Chaucer Manuscripts complete Dd.4.24 diplomatic transcription
entities.description: The complete downloadable XML transcription bundle produced by the Chaucer Manuscripts project. It includes Dd.4.24 fols. 105v–106r in l15-1.xml and l15-2.xml for Furnivall's Merchant's Prologue supply, and f. 147r in pd-11.xml for his Pardoner's Tale supply.

entities.label: Thomas Tyrwhitt's 1775–1778 Canterbury Tales edition
entities.description: The eighteenth-century edition whose paragraph divisions Furnivall says he retained for the prose Tale of Melibee and Parson's Tale. The exact volume and paragraph loci have not yet been frozen in this manifest.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer, ed. Thomas Tyrwhitt (London, 1775–1778).
bibliographic.title: The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer

entities.label: Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9 (Ellesmere Chaucer)
entities.description: A deluxe illuminated manuscript of The Canterbury Tales produced in England around 1400–1410. Furnivall's visual appendix uses it for twenty-three teller designs; it is a collateral visual source here, not Furnivall's textual base witness.

holding.notes: The physical manuscript is restricted; Huntington directs researchers to digital reproductions or the print facsimile.

physical_description.extent: 232 main text folios plus twelve cataloged non-text leaves; 480 canvases in the current published IIIF sequence

entities.label: Huntington 480-canvas published IIIF presentation of the Ellesmere Chaucer
entities.description: The complete official provider-published 480-canvas sequence. It reproduces all 232 main text folios and eight preliminary/final leaves; four additional non-text leaves in the physical extent statement are not in the published sequence.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

editorial_practices.source_feature: A damaged principal Gg.4.27(1) text with lost leaves, incomplete links, and other gaps, alongside readings in multiple supplementary witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: A principally Gg text whose explicitly marked gaps and comparison passages are supplied from Harley 1758, Sloane 1685, Dd.4.24, Harley 7335, Royal 18 C II, Royal 17 D XV, Hatton donat. 1, and one unresolved Z/E/I siglum
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's printed text is a documented composite, not a continuous diplomatic transcription of Gg.4.27(1). The passage-scoped transmission relations enumerate every source run found in the edition and XML.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete printed Canterbury Tales text and Gamelyn appendix, with each source transition bounded by Furnivall's notes and the XML source-switch audit

editorial_practices.source_feature: Gg.4.27(1) is a larger anthology, and The Canterbury Tales survives in ten internally linked but not securely author-ordered fragments with textual losses
editorial_practices.representation: A one-text Canterbury Tales edition in Furnivall's ten-group Gg sequence, followed by the non-Chaucerian Gamelyn and textual and visual appendices, while the rest of the Gg anthology is omitted
editorial_practices.description: The edition's carrier, sequence, supplements, and appendices are editorial choices. They should not be projected backward as the whole Gg codex or a final authorial order of The Canterbury Tales.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript prose divisions in the Tale of Melibee and the Parson's Tale
editorial_practices.representation: Numbered prose paragraphs retained from Tyrwhitt's eighteenth-century edition
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall states that he follows Tyrwhitt's paragraphing for the two prose tales. These paragraph numbers are an inherited editorial navigation layer, not manuscript foliation.
editorial_practices.scope: Tale of Melibee and Parson's Tale

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete printed carrier containing preliminaries, the edited text, appendices, illustrations, and other back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected Level 1 OCR encoding of the title page, ten main groups, Gamelyn, and selected notices across 738 linked page images, with broad front and back divisions explicitly marked omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding is not a transcription of every printed surface. The repository artifact inherits this selection and the OCR's source-note representation.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, order images, image-order form, and current linked reuse terms.
evidence.locator: request eligibility, prices, turnaround, order submission, shelfmark/folio requirements, supplied-image use, and reproduction conditions
evidence.summary: The official service accepts image requests for collection items not available through Digital Bodleian or Bodleian Images. It lists a £20 minimum, £27 for the first high-resolution colour image and £7.50 for subsequent images from the same item, negotiated pricing for large orders, and a standard 20-working-day turnaround after payment. Orders require the exact shelfmark and folio/page and may be submitted by email or post. The linked order and reuse documents support noncommercial research use and refer to CC BY-NC 4.0 for out-of-copyright scans, while separately linked image-order conditions require written permission for reproduction; item-specific delivered terms must therefore be retained and checked before redistribution or commercial use.

evidence.citation: United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright (term).
